---
name: Bug report
about: Something in W.A.Y? behaves differently from what the docs or rules describe
labels: bug
---

<!--
Thanks for helping make W.A.Y? more reliable. W.A.Y? is built for people who are
"not developers," so plain-language reports are completely fine — no need to phrase
this like a developer would. Fill in what you can; partial reports still help.
-->

## What happened

A clear, plain-language description of the problem. If the harness made a factual
claim you suspect is wrong, or skipped one of its honesty checks, say so here.

## Steps to reproduce

1.
2.
3.

<!-- If a specific instruction or prompt triggered it, paste the exact wording. -->

## Expected behavior

What you thought W.A.Y? would do — ideally pointing at the rule or doc that sets
that expectation (for example, a line in `CLAUDE.md`, a file under `rules/`, or
`harness-blueprint.md`).

## Actual behavior

What W.A.Y? actually did instead.

## Environment

- **CLI agent**: <!-- e.g. Claude Code, or another CLI agent -->
- **OS**: <!-- e.g. macOS 15, Ubuntu 24.04, Windows 11 -->
- **W.A.Y? version / commit**: <!-- from CHANGELOG.md or `git rev-parse --short HEAD` -->
- **Optional plugins installed**: <!-- e.g. insane-search, deep-research, or none -->

## Additional context

Logs, screenshots, or anything else that helps.

> **Please do not paste personal information or real operational data** (real names,
> emails, private numbers, customer data, contents of git-isolated folders). W.A.Y?
> keeps operational data isolated by design (principle P6) — please keep this issue
> the same way. Redact before posting.
